user_message: Can you extract the whitespace analysis from this exec meeting notes for Consolidated Messenger? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-02-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Consolidated Messenger)

[00:02:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Priya (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:16:00] Priya (Consolidated Messenger): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:19:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Priya (Consolidated Messenger): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:26:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-I trap (Decision log misses dissenting voices); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-I failure mode (Decision log misses dissenting voices)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-I -- Decision log misses dissenting voices
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
